english: Qin Shi Huang (first emperor of Qin)

etymology.description: 秦(秦) (qín, "Qin dynasty") + 始(始) (shǐ, "beginning") + 皇(皇) (huáng, "emperor")
etymology.interpretation: 秦(秦) (qín, "Qin dynasty") refers to the state and dynasty that ultimately unified China. 始(始) (shǐ, "beginning") marks the commencement of the imperial rule under the unification. 皇(皇) (huáng, "emperor") indicates the supreme ruler. Together, the title 秦始皇 (Qín Shǐhuáng) identifies the first emperor of the Qin dynasty, who began the imperial era by centralizing power.

examples.chinese: 秦始皇创造了中国的第一个中央集权国家(秦始皇創造了中國的第一個中央集權國家)。
examples.pinyin: Qín Shǐhuáng chuàngzàole Zhōngguó de dì yī gè zhōngyāng jíquán guójiā.
examples.english: Qin Shi Huang created the first centralized government in China.

examples.chinese: 秦始皇下令修建万里长城(秦始皇下令修建萬里長城)。
examples.pinyin: Qín Shǐhuáng xiàlìng xiūjiàn Wànlǐ Chángchéng.
examples.english: Qin Shi Huang ordered the construction of the Great Wall.

examples.chinese: 很多历史故事都围绕秦始皇展开(很多歷史故事都圍繞秦始皇展開)。
examples.pinyin: Hěn duō lìshǐ gùshì dōu wéirào Qín Shǐhuáng zhǎnkāi.
examples.english: Many historical stories revolve around Qin Shi Huang.
